Summary: Assignment process starts automatically after a valid rank is saved
TestSteps: Take a classified estate with a saved rank (A or D) and no active assignment request
ExpectedResults: An assignment request is created for the estate and the estate moves out of the pre-assignment state
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Trigger/observe the assignment process and query cases.liquidator_assignment_requests for the estate

Summary: Only services with the Liquidator role are considered as candidates
TestSteps: Inspect the candidate pool used for selection on a classified estate
ExpectedResults: Only accepted Liquidator services are considered; non-liquidator services are excluded
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Compare the eligible-candidate set against facility services by type

Summary: Inactive liquidator accounts are excluded from selection
TestSteps: Ensure a liquidator service whose account is inactive exists
ExpectedResults: The inactive liquidator is never selected; only active-account liquidators are eligible
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm the inactive liquidator is not chosen
Use a rank-A estate with an available rank-A liquidator service (Majed NID 1100000011)
ExpectedResults: The rank-A liquidator is selected for the rank-A inheritance (same-rank match)
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm the selected liquidator_user_id matches the rank-A service

Summary: Higher-rank escalation when no same-rank liquidator exists
TestSteps: Use a rank-D estate when no rank-D/C/B liquidator exists but a rank-A liquidator does
ExpectedResults: The system escalates D->C->B->A and selects the rank-A liquidator (never a lower rank)
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm the higher-rank liquidator is selected

Summary: Never assign a lower-ranked liquidator to a higher-ranked inheritance
TestSteps: Arrange an estate whose rank is higher than an available liquidator's rank only
ExpectedResults: The lower-ranked liquidator is not selected; if none eligible, the process stops (no wrong assignment)
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm no lower-rank assignment occurred

Summary: Workload priority selects the liquidator with the lowest count
TestSteps: Provision two eligible liquidators of the same rank with different workloads (accepted + pending)
ExpectedResults: The liquidator with the lowest (accepted + pending) count is selected
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm the lowest-workload liquidator was chosen

Summary: Tie-break selects the next liquidator in the queue
TestSteps: Provision two eligible same-rank liquidators with equal lowest workload
ExpectedResults: On a tie the system selects the next one in the queue (deterministic order)
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm the queue-order tie-break

Summary: Inheritance status becomes «قيد تعيين المصفي» after the request is sent
TestSteps: Run assignment on a classified estate
ExpectedResults: The stored inheritance status is set to «قيد تعيين المصفي» after a successful send
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Query the estate status in the DB after assignment

Summary: One inheritance-log entry «إرسال طلب تعيين للمصفي» is created
TestSteps: Run assignment on a classified estate
ExpectedResults: Exactly one log entry is created with event «إرسال طلب تعيين للمصفي», Done By = النظام, and View-More showing selected liquidator facility, rank, and action status
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Query the inheritance log / case_events for the estate

Summary: Assignment request appears in the liquidator's list with قبول/تعذر
TestSteps: Log in as the selected liquidator (Nafath NID 1100000011)
ExpectedResults: The estate appears in the liquidator's inheritance list with قبول (accept) and تعذر (reject) actions
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Open the liquidator court-cases list and locate the assigned estate

Summary: No assignment starts when the rank is not saved
TestSteps: Use an estate whose classification did not produce a valid rank (no rank saved)
ExpectedResults: The assignment process does not start; no assignment request is created
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Confirm no row in liquidator_assignment_requests for that estate

Summary: Duplicate / overlapping assignment requests are prevented
TestSteps: Take an estate that already has one active (pending) assignment request
ExpectedResults: A second active request is not created while one is still pending
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Attempt to trigger assignment again and confirm only one active request exists

Summary: 48-hour no-response triggers reassignment and excludes the non-responder
TestSteps: Take an estate with a pending request; age the request past 48h (DB timestamp)
ExpectedResults: Reassignment starts automatically and the non-responding liquidator is excluded from re-selection for the same estate
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run the timeout/reassignment job and confirm exclusion + new request

Summary: A rejected/excluded liquidator is not selected again for the same inheritance
TestSteps: Mark a liquidator as excluded (rejected/timed-out) for an estate
ExpectedResults: The same liquidator is never re-selected for that estate on reassignment
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Trigger reassignment and confirm the excluded liquidator is skipped

Summary: No eligible liquidator at all stops the process cleanly
TestSteps: Arrange an estate for which no eligible (active + rank-adequate) liquidator exists
ExpectedResults: The automatic assignment stops without error and no request is created (manager-notify handling is out of scope)
Creator: Ahmad Altwaam

TestSteps: Run assignment and confirm no request + no crash
